untranslatable_reason: Signal requires dynamic Kelly formula calculation based on trailing mean return and standard deviation with progressive leverage adjustment. The allowed indicator set does not include: (1) Kelly formula computation, (2) trailing mean return calculation, (3) trailing standard deviation calculation, (4) dynamic leverage multiplier adjustment mechanism. These require custom statistical computations and portfolio-level leverage control not expressible as simple binary conditions on the provided indicators.
translation_notes: This is a portfolio-level risk management rule rather than a traditional entry/exit signal. It requires: (1) Computing Kelly criterion: f* = (mean_return - risk_free_rate) / variance, (2) Calculating trailing statistics over AUTHOR_SILENT lookback period, (3) Adjusting leverage from current level toward zero as performance deteriorates. The rule is regime-aware (applies during declining performance) but cannot be reduced to indicator threshold conditions. Implementation requires custom risk engine capable of: tracking cumulative returns, computing rolling volatility, calculating Kelly ratios, and modulating position sizing dynamically. Recommend implementing as separate leverage control module outside standard backtest rule engine.
